run_summary: Screen 1 flags two OVERDONE positions with confidence 3 — one partial panic (DUOL) and one conviction-grade sympathy fade (PLAB). Portfolio is fully cash; both trades are eligible, but tier assignments differ sharply.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI claims it solved an 80-year-old math problem — for real this time
entries.thesis: DUOL is the reflexive edtech sell on any OpenAI capability headline, but today's trigger is a math-reasoning demo, not a consumer language-learning product. DUOL's moat is the daily-habit dopamine/streak loop and gamification, not instructional content quality — areas a reasoning model breakthrough does not directly attack. The selloff is partial overshoot relative to the actual threat substance.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that an OpenAI consumer-facing tutor product is shipping with language-learning features; meaningful DAU decline in the next print; Apple/Google App Store featuring competing AI tutors prominently.
entries.reasoning: Confidence 3 meets min_buy_confidence, and classification is OVERDONE, but panic_calibration is 'partial' (not 'unjustified'), and threat_assessment is 'indirect' (not 'none' or 'minimal'). Filings evidence cuts both ways: risk factors acknowledge GenAI disruption threat explicitly, but DUOL's own Max product launch shows active defense. The sympathy-fade thesis is muddled — there is a real competitive vector here, just not from a math-reasoning demo. Not a clean exploratory-tier entry. WATCH and reassess if threat weakens or panic recalibrates.
